page.title: Marbella Boat Rental with Catering: Onboard Dining Options

data.meta_description: Discover marbella boat rental with catering for 5–12 guests. From tapas to paella, we serve onboard dining. Book your charter with full catering today.
data.summary: Explore marbella boat rental with catering options for groups of 5 to 12. Our charters include tapas, paella, and custom menus, all served onboard by your skipper.
key_facts: All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance.
Catering options range from tapas and paella to custom menus (pricing on request).
Fleet accommodates 5–12 guests; boats depart from Puerto Banús.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749/2h to €2,299/8h.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h, includes a free jet ski.
Catering is arranged via WhatsApp; we coordinate with local caterers.
Popular menus: Spanish tapas, grilled seafood, paella, and vegetarian options.
Dietary requirements (allergies, halal, vegan) can be accommodated with notice.

faq.q: What catering options are available for a marbella boat rental with catering?
faq.a: We offer a range of catering options including Spanish tapas, paella, grilled seafood, cold cuts, and vegetarian platters. Custom menus are available on request via WhatsApp. All catering is served onboard by your skipper, with drinks and snacks already included in your charter.

faq.q: How do I add catering to my boat rental?
faq.a: Simply contact us via WhatsApp after booking or during the booking process. We will coordinate with our local caterers to arrange your preferred menu, dietary requirements, and timing. Catering is an add-on service; pricing depends on the menu and number of guests.

faq.q: Can you accommodate dietary restrictions or allergies?
faq.a: Yes. Please inform us of any allergies, intolerances, or dietary preferences (halal, vegan, gluten-free) when you book. We will work with our caterers to ensure your meal is safe and enjoyable. Additional charges may apply for special requests.

faq.q: Is catering included in the standard boat rental price?
faq.a: No. The standard charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Catering is an optional extra that you can add for a fee. Contact us for a quote.

faq.q: What types of cuisine can I choose from?
faq.a: We specialize in Mediterranean and Spanish cuisine. Popular choices include seafood paella, grilled prawns, jamón ibérico, manchego cheese, patatas bravas, and fresh salads. We can also arrange international options like sushi or BBQ upon request.

faq.q: How far in advance do I need to order catering?
faq.a: We recommend ordering at least 48 hours before your charter to ensure availability and preparation. For large groups or custom menus, earlier notice is appreciated. Last-minute requests may be possible—contact us via WhatsApp to check.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.a: You are welcome to bring your own food and drink, but please note that our standard charter already includes drinks and snacks. If you prefer a full meal, we recommend using our catering service to ensure quality and convenience.

faq.q: Do you offer catering for larger groups on bigger boats?
faq.a: Yes. Our larger boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) and Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) can accommodate full catering for up to 12 guests. We can arrange multi-course meals, buffet-style, or plated service. Contact us for a tailored quote.

data.body_html:
When you book a marbella boat rental with catering, you get more than just a day on the water—you get a floating restaurant experience. Whether you're celebrating a birthday, impressing clients, or simply enjoying the Costa del Sol with friends, onboard dining elevates the trip. Our fleet of 17 boats departs from Puerto Banús and accommodates 5 to 12 guests. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Add a custom menu—tapas, paella, grilled seafood—and you have a meal to match the views.

Why choose a boat rental with catering in Marbella?

Eating onboard saves you time and hassle. No need to dock at a crowded chiringuito or rush back to port. Your skipper anchors in a calm cove—like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés—and serves your meal while you swim or sunbathe. The water is warm (22–26°C in summer), the sea breeze keeps things fresh, and the backdrop of La Concha mountain makes every bite memorable. Catering also lets you control the menu: dietary needs, portion sizes, and timing are all sorted before you leave.

What catering options are available?

We work with local caterers who specialise in Mediterranean and Spanish cuisine. Standard options include:

  • Spanish tapas (jamón ibérico, manchego, patatas bravas, croquetas)
  • Seafood platters (grilled prawns, octopus, mussels)
  • Paella (seafood, chicken, or vegetarian)
  • Cold cuts and cheese boards
  • Fresh salads and grilled vegetables
  • Desserts (crema catalana, fresh fruit)

Custom menus—sushi, BBQ, or international dishes—are available on request. Pricing depends on the menu and number of guests; contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

Which boats are best for a catered charter?

All our boats can accommodate catering, but larger vessels offer more space for dining. Here are popular choices:

BoatLengthMax paxPrice (from)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4h
Ferretti 9429m12Request via WhatsApp
Canados 8626m12Request via WhatsApp

For smaller groups, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) work well for an intimate tapas lunch. All depart from Puerto Banús.

How to book a marbella boat rental with catering

Booking is simple. Choose your boat and time slot (2h, 4h, 6h, or 8h). Then message us on WhatsApp with your preferred menu and any dietary requirements. We’ll confirm availability and provide a price. Payment is handled securely, and your skipper will have everything ready when you board. For last-minute bookings, we recommend calling ahead—48 hours notice for catering is ideal, but we can sometimes accommodate sooner.

Read more about our full fleet and charter options.

Sample itinerary for a catered day charter

A typical 8-hour charter (10:00–18:00) might look like this:

  • 10:00 – Board your boat at Puerto Banús. Meet your skipper, stow your bags.
  • 10:30 – Depart towards Cabopino or Cala del Faro. Enjoy drinks and light snacks.
  • 12:00 – Anchor for swimming. Skipper sets up the catering—tapas and paella served.
  • 14:00 – Cruise along the coast towards Estepona or Sotogrande.
  • 16:00 – Second swim stop. Dessert and coffee served.
  • 17:30 – Return to Puerto Banús. Disembark.

Shorter charters (2–4 hours) can include a lighter menu—tapas and drinks—while longer trips allow for a full multi-course meal.

What’s included in every charter?

All our rentals include: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). Catering is an optional extra. You can also bring your own food, but we recommend our catering for quality and convenience.

Frequently asked questions

What catering options are available for a marbella boat rental with catering?

We offer a range of catering options including Spanish tapas, paella, grilled seafood, cold cuts, and vegetarian platters. Custom menus are available on request via WhatsApp. All catering is served onboard by your skipper, with drinks and snacks already included in your charter.

How do I add catering to my boat rental?

Simply contact us via WhatsApp after booking or during the booking process. We will coordinate with our local caterers to arrange your preferred menu, dietary requirements, and timing. Catering is an add-on service; pricing depends on the menu and number of guests.

Can you accommodate dietary restrictions or allergies?

Yes. Please inform us of any allergies, intolerances, or dietary preferences (halal, vegan, gluten-free) when you book. We will work with our caterers to ensure your meal is safe and enjoyable. Additional charges may apply for special requests.

Is catering included in the standard boat rental price?

No. The standard charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Catering is an optional extra that you can add for a fee. Contact us for a quote.

What types of cuisine can I choose from?

We specialize in Mediterranean and Spanish cuisine. Popular choices include seafood paella, grilled prawns, jamón ibérico, manchego cheese, patatas bravas, and fresh salads. We can also arrange international options like sushi or BBQ upon request.

How far in advance do I need to order catering?

We recommend ordering at least 48 hours before your charter to ensure availability and preparation. For large groups or custom menus, earlier notice is appreciated. Last-minute requests may be possible—contact us via WhatsApp to check.

Can I bring my own food and drink?

You are welcome to bring your own food and drink, but please note that our standard charter already includes drinks and snacks. If you prefer a full meal, we recommend using our catering service to ensure quality and convenience.

Do you offer catering for larger groups on bigger boats?

Yes. Our larger boats like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) and Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) can accommodate full catering for up to 12 guests. We can arrange multi-course meals, buffet-style, or plated service. Contact us for a tailored quote.
